title: Yosys emits assigns with a concatenated expression on the LHS
body:
Yosys can emit verilog that has a concatenated signal as the recipient of an assign statement.

I.e something like:

`assign {a,b} = {c,d};`

The problem is that this causes problems for other tools; in this case OpenSTA [*], which means I can't properly verify my design.

I did not see this issue until recently, and it occurred after I introduced a `hilomap` stage, possibly because that reduces the fanin of constant buses. It's difficult to provide an example because it only seems to happen under certain circumstances.

I did try adding some `opt_clean` and `clean` stages after the `hilomap`, but that didn't seem to help.

I think I saw a previous issue/communication about this somewhere (but can't find it anymore ..), where it was stated that this was legal verilog, and therefore should be OK. Well, I can see that point of view, but it just seems it would cause less trouble if such assigns were split into multiple assigns with only one signal as their recipient.

[*] OpenSTA is really great, by the way.
